bead.title: tests: move internal/server/perf behind //go:build perf tag; add make test-perf target
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  The cli/internal/server/perf package contains performance benchmarks
  that run for ~80 seconds during the default lefthook pre-push
  full-tests step. These are perf tests by intent, not unit tests; they
  should not block every push. Today they have no build tag or skip
  gate, so they run on every full-test invocation.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/server/perf/ contains benchmark tests (e.g.
    TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline) that take ~80s when not skipped.
  - The package has a testing.Short() skip in some tests but not all,
    and pre-push runs without -short.
  - Makefile has a bench-graphql target (lines ~121-127) that writes a
    Markdown baseline report when DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR is set, but it is
    not invoked from CI today.
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml does not invoke `make bench-graphql` or
    otherwise run the perf package as a dedicated step.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add `//go:build perf` tag to every test file in
    cli/internal/server/perf/. Files to edit (run `ls
    cli/internal/server/perf/*_test.go` and tag each).
  - Update Makefile:
      a. Add a `test-perf` target that runs `go test -tags=perf
         ./internal/server/perf/...` with an appropriate timeout (e.g.
         5 minutes).
      b. Document that `make test` excludes perf; `make test-perf` runs
         them.
  - Update .github/workflows/ci.yml:
      a. Add a new step or job that runs `make test-perf` on a schedule
         OR on pull requests touching cli/internal/server/perf/. Don't
         run on every PR by default; perf is non-gating.
  - Update lefthook.yml ci block (or full-tests step) only if it
    explicitly references perf (it doesn't appear to).
  - Verify default `cd cli && go test ./...` no longer pulls perf tests
    in (they should be excluded by the build tag).

NON-SCOPE
  - Other test packages outside cli/internal/server/perf/ (covered by
    sister beads).
  - Designing a perf-regression dashboard (separate concern; this bead
    just gates the slow tests behind a tag).
  - Removing the perf tests; they remain valuable, just not on the
    default path.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead: "lefthook pre-push: add -short gate" (independent — the
    -short flip and the perf-tag move both reduce pre-push time, by
    different mechanisms; they compose).
bead.acceptance:
1. Every *_test.go file in cli/internal/server/perf/ has a //go:build perf tag at the top.
2. cd cli && go test ./... (no -tags=perf) reports zero tests run from cli/internal/server/perf/...
3. cd cli && go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/... runs the perf tests as before.
4. Makefile contains a test-perf target invoking the tagged build.
5. .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a step or job that invokes make test-perf on an appropriate trigger (schedule, perf-touching PR, or manual workflow dispatch).
6. cd cli && go test ./... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
